evidence: All 105 milestone_record tests pass (0 failures, 0 ignored) — verified by running cargo test --lib contexts::milestone_record
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes cleanly (no warnings)
model.rs:22-31 MilestoneId::new() correctly rejects dot-prefixed identifiers via starts_with('.'); the redundant == '.' and == '..' checks have been removed as they are subsumed by starts_with('.')
service.rs:111-123 TaskRunLineagePort::update_task_run now accepts started_at: DateTime<Utc> at line 119, enabling runless same-project retry disambiguation
service.rs:465-495 record_bead_completion now accepts started_at as a real parameter (line 477, not underscore-prefixed) and forwards it to update_task_run (line 490)
fs.rs:2558-2566 running_task_runs_for_bead filters by bead_id only (not project_id), enabling cross-project superseding of stale running attempts for the same bead
fs.rs:2823-2824 fail_superseded_running_attempt is now called unconditionally for any sole prior running attempt (not just legacy runless ones), supporting cross-project retry scenarios

fs.rs:2977-3038 runless update_task_run branch now narrows by started_at first (line 2981: entries[*index].started_at == started_at), preventing ambiguous matching when multiple runless retries exist for the same bead/project

Test runless_completion_replay_for_same_project_retry_repairs_journal (service.rs:3159-3283) verifies: two runless starts for same project, complete second, strip journal completion, replay completion with retry's started_at — asserts 1 BeadCompleted event and snapshot completed_beads=1, failed_beads=0

Test retry_start_on_same_bead_supersedes_running_attempt_from_other_project (service.rs:3534-3633) verifies: start bead-1/project-1/run-1, then start bead-1/project-2/run-2 — first attempt auto-failed with 'superseded by retry', second is Running, after completion snapshot shows Ready with completed_beads=1
All mutation paths (update_status, persist_plan, record_bead_start, update_task_run) remain under with_milestone_write_lock for serialized access
reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage rebuilds all counters from latest_task_runs_per_bead, preserves Paused status, transitions Active→Ready when no active beads remain
findings_or_gaps: The outstanding risk — milestone mutations span separate lineage, snapshot, and journal files without a cross-file transaction — remains. All paths are serialized by the milestone mutation lock, replay-safe via started_at-based targeting, and auto-supersede stale running attempts, making this an acceptable trade-off for file-based storage.

AppError::RunStartFailed is still used (service.rs:549) for rejecting non-terminal outcomes in update_task_run, which is semantically a finalization rejection. A more specific error variant would be cleaner but this is cosmetic, not a correctness issue — carried forward from prior reviews.

The asymmetry between project-scoped idempotency check (find_matching_running_task_run filters by bead+project+run_id) and bead-scoped supersede check (running_task_runs_for_bead filters by bead only) is intentional for cross-project retry support but non-obvious to future maintainers. A brief inline comment at fs.rs:2823 explaining the rationale would help.

The active_bead_ids() function in model.rs:402-408 still contains its own internal latest_task_runs_per_bead() call while reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage computes latest runs separately. External callers of active_bead_ids would redundantly recompute, but this is minor and not a correctness concern.
